channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Bank of America is having service issues and account access problems: Downdetector
item.description: A message on the Bank of America app states, “Accounts Temporarily Unavailable. Some accounts and/or balances are temporarily unavailable.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, NewsNation

item.title: Oasis adds US, Canada and Mexico stops to reunion tour
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Oasis shocked fans by announcing a reunion tour last month, ending a 15-year hiatus and, presumably, the long-held feud between brothers Liam and Noel Gallagher. But those were only U.K. dates. On Monday, the band shared that they're planning on heading to North America in 2025, too.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Catholic reform debate launched by Pope Francis leans away from ordained roles for women
item.description: ROME (AP) — Debate was leaning away from allowing women to take on ordained roles in the Catholic Church as the second phase of Pope Francis’ reform project opened Wednesday with an agenda topped by calls for women to take up more positions of responsibility Francis presided over an opening Mass in
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Police delivered a 'beatdown' that killed Tyre Nichols, prosecutor says in trial closing
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Memphis police officers who beat Tyre Nichols to death wanted to punish him after he ran from a 2023 traffic stop and thought they could get away with it, a prosecutor said Wednesday as closing arguments began in the federal trial of three of the officers.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Hurricane Helene's victims include first responders who died helping others
item.description: Falling trees and raging floods from Hurricane Helene killed more than 150 people when it struck Florida late last week and then plowed through the Southeast as one of the deadliest storms in U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: BRENDAN FARRINGTON and DAVID FISCHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Harris and Biden head to the Southeast to see Helene's damage amid high political stakes
item.description: GREENVILLE, S.C. (AP) — President Joe Biden toured some of the areas hardest-hit by Hurricane Helene via helicopter on Wednesday as he and Vice President Kamala Harris traveled to different parts of the Southeast to get first-hand looks at the devastation.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal, state officials announce arrest of 68 members of neo-Nazi group in Los Angeles
item.description: A total of 68 people were indicted as part of the “Peckerwood gang,” Estrada confirmed. Of the 68, a total of 42 are in custody.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, NewsNation

item.title: After employees swept away in Helene flooding, Tennessee factory under investigation
item.description: Several employees have accused the company of not letting them leave soon enough as flooding set in on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Keeling, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tribes celebrate the end of the largest dam removal project in US history
item.description: The largest dam removal project in U.S. history was completed Wednesday, marking a major victory for tribes in the region who fought for decades to free hundreds of miles of the Klamath River near the California-Oregon border.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden won't support a strike on Iran nuclear sites as Israel weighs response to Iran missile attack
item.description: JOINT BASE ANDREWS, Md. (AP) — President Joe Biden said Wednesday he will not support an Israeli strike on sites related to Tehran’s nuclear program in response to Iran's missile attack on Israel.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: California Gov. Newsom signs bills to protect children from AI deepfake nudes
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom signed legislation on Sunday to help protect minors from harmful sexual imagery of children created through the misuse of artificial intelligence tools.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shock of deadly floods is a reminder of Appalachia’s risk from violent storms in a warming climate
item.description: HENDERSONVILLE, N.C. (AP) — Hurricane Helene dumped trillions of gallons of water hundreds of miles inland, devastating communities nestled in mountains far from the threat of storm surge or sea level rise.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump rejects calls for another debate with Harris after VP showdown
item.description: Former President Donald Trump again rejected the prospect of another debate with Vice President Kamala Harris late Tuesday following a showdown between the two vice presidential nominees, saying he was "not looking to do it again.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pennsylvania Republican in key swing-state Senate race backs using military to fight fentanyl
item.description: STEELTON, Pa. (AP) — The Republican challenger trying to flip the U.S. Senate seat in swing-state Pennsylvania said he’ll press for U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US 'Welcome Corps' helps resettle LGBTQ+ refugees fleeing crackdowns against gay people
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Cabrel Ngounou's life in Cameroon quickly unraveled after neighbors caught the teenager with his boyfriend. A crowd surrounded his boyfriend's house and beat him. Ngounou's family learned of the relationship and kicked him out.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and TERRY CHEA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Prosecutors say they may bring additional charges against New York City mayor and indict others
item.description: Federal prosecutors say they might bring additional charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams and indict others in the corruption case against him.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Impact Plastics employee recounts narrow escape through floodwater
item.description: "We were all talking to the supervisors and telling everybody, 'Look, we don't need to be here,'" Adkins recalled.
item.dc:creator: Karen Jenkins, NewsNation

item.title: School board to pay $575K to teacher fired for not using transgender student's pronouns
item.description: A Virginia school board has agreed to pay a former high school teacher more than $500,000 in damages and attorneys' fees as part of a legal settlement after the instructor's dismissal in 2018 for refusing to use a transgender student's pronouns.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KRON4

item.title: 18-year-old charged in shooting death of dad in Adrian, Missouri
item.description: According to arrest reports, Carter J. Curtis faces one count of first-degree murder and one count of armed criminal action for allegedly shooting his father with a rifle.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: WWII destroyer found off California coast 76 years later
item.description: A surviving ship from WWII that was used by both Allied and Axis forces in the Pacific and that was given a ceremonial burial at sea has finally been located after more than seven decades underwater.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Nobert, KRON4

item.title: John Amos, patriarch on 'Good Times' and an Emmy nominee for the blockbuster 'Roots,' dies at 84
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — John Amos, who starred as the family patriarch on the hit 1970s sitcom “Good Times" and earned an Emmy nomination for his role in the seminal 1977 miniseries “Roots,” has died. He was 84. He died Aug. 21 of natural causes in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: McDonald’s is bringing back its famous ‘Boo Buckets,’ with a new color
item.description: McDonald's famous Boo Buckets are making a return just in time for Halloween, and a new color has joined the lineup this year.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, ABC 4

item.title: Why Tuesday's vice presidential debate could matter more than history suggests
item.description: Tim Walz and JD Vance might have the chance to reshape the political landscape Tuesday in their first and only debate.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Helene's death toll passes 150 as crews search for survivors in North Carolina's mountains
item.description: Cadaver dogs and search crews trudged through knee-deep muck and debris on Tuesday looking in the mountains of western North Carolina for victims of Hurricane Helene, days after the storm carved a deadly and destructive path through the Southeast.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Iran fires at least 180 missiles into Israel as regionwide conflict grows
item.description: Iran launched at least 180 missiles into Israel on Tuesday, the latest in a series of escalating attacks in a yearslong conflict between Israel and Iran and its Arab allies that threatens to push the Middle East closer toward a regionwide war.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Fox 5

item.title: NHC tracking disturbance near where Helene formed | Tracking the Tropics
item.description: How strong it will be and where is it going to go? This afternoon on Tracking the Tropics, we'll break down what we know about the disturbance and where it could be headed.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: My car was submerged in floodwaters. Should I start it?
item.description: Storm surges and flooding from Hurricane Helene have caused widespread devastation across parts of the Southeastern U.S., in some cases leaving homes and cars completely underwater.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Israel says Iran has fired missiles at Israel and it warned residents to shelter in place
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — The Israeli military said Tuesday that Iran has fired missiles at Israel, and air raid sirens sounded across the country as residents were ordered to remain close to bomb shelters. Israel and the United States have warned there would be severe consequences if Iran attacks.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, JULIA FRANKEL and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Crews search for survivors in North Carolina's mountains days after Helene's deluge
item.description: SWANNANOA, N.C. (AP) — Rescuers fanned out across the mountains of western North Carolina on Tuesday in search of anyone still unaccounted for since Hurricane Helene’s remnants caused catastrophic damage to the Southeast, with a death toll nearing 140 people.
item.dc:creator: ERIK VERDUZCO and JEFFREY COLLINS Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Are professional athletes playing too many games?
item.description: Fans, athletes and medical experts have been having discussions on the increasing demand for players starting from the time they first start playing, all the way up to the professional ranks.
item.dc:creator: Juan Cisneros, Fox 5

item.title: Helene's death toll surpasses 150 as crews continue to search for survivors
item.description: Exhausted emergency workers continued to work around-the-clock to clear roads, restore power and cellphone service, and reach people left stranded by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: PlayStation Network reports global outage
item.description: PlayStation users are facing widespread outages across multiple platforms, leaving many unable to sign in or access online features.
item.dc:creator: Tor Smith, Fox 5

item.title: A century of Jimmy Carter: Longest-living former president turns 100
item.description: In 1998, at the ripe age of 74, former President Carter put some of his thoughts about getting older to paper. "The virtues of aging," Carter wrote in a book that year, "include both the blessings that come to us as we grow older and what we have to offer that might be beneficial to others.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: These are the 50 best school districts in America for 2025, report finds
item.description: Choosing where to live could come down to the strength of the school district for many parents, and a new study ranks the top 50 in the nation.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Gavin Creel, Tony Award-winning veteran of Broadway musicals, dies at 48
item.description: Creel's publicist said he died at his home in Manhattan of metastatic melanotic peripheral nerve sheath sarcoma.
item.dc:creator: Mark Kennedy, Fox 5

item.title: DirecTV buys rival Dish TV and Sling for just $1
item.description: DirecTV is buying Dish and Sling, a deal it has sought to complete for years, as the company seeks to better compete against streaming services that have become dominant.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Supplies arrive by plane and mule as Helene death toll passes 100
item.description: Desperate residents isolated by washed-out roads and the lack of power and cellular service in western North Carolina lined up Monday for fresh water and a chance to message loved ones that they were alive.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4
